g.text: MN 106 — ĀNEÑJASAPPĀYA SUTTA
Conducive to the Imperturbable
"The crossing of the flood by relying on one support after another"
nissāya nissāya oghassa nittharaṇā
CONDUCIVE TO THE IMPERTURBABLE · āneñjasappāyā paṭipadā
REFLECTION
MĀRA'S BAIT · mārassesa nivāpo·MĀRA'S HUNTING GROUND · mārassesa gocaro
"Sensual pleasures are impermanent, hollow, false, deceptive — they are created by illusion, the prattle of fools"
aniccā kāmā tucchā musā mosadhammā — māyākatametaṁ bālalāpanaṁ
On account of them, these harmful (pāpakā) and unwholesome (akusalā) states arise:
Intense longing
abhijjhā
Ill will
byāpāda
Aggressiveness
sārambha
1ST WAY
dwell thus often
tabbahulavihārī
Having surpassed the world, dwell with an abundant and exalted mind
vipulena mahaggatena cetasā abhibhuyya lokaṁ adhiṭṭhāya manasā
By abandoning harmful states, the mind becomes vast, boundless, and well cultivated
aparittaṁ appamāṇaṁ subhāvitaṁ
When the mind acquires confidence · he attains to the imperturbable
āyatane cittaṁ pasīdati · āneñjaṁ samāpajjati
2ND WAY
dwell thus often
tabbahulavihārī
Whatever form there is, all form is the four great elements
and form derived from the four great elements
yaṁ kiñci rūpaṁ sabbaṁ rūpaṁ cattāri ca mahābhūtāni, catunnañca mahābhūtānaṁ upādāyarūpa
When the mind acquires confidence · he attains to the imperturbable
āyatane cittaṁ pasīdati · āneñjaṁ samāpajjati
3RD WAY
dwell thus often
tabbahulavihārī
Sensuality, forms, and their perceptions — both are impermanent
kāmā, kāmasaññā, rūpā, rūpasaññā — ubhayametaṁ aniccaṁ
What is impermanent is not worth delighting in, not worth welcoming, not worth clinging to
yadiccaṁ taṁ nālaṁ abhinandituṁ, nālaṁ abhivadituṁ, nālaṁ ajjhosituṁ
When the mind acquires confidence · he attains to the imperturbable
āyatane cittaṁ pasīdati · āneñjaṁ samāpajjati
CONDUCIVE TO THE BASE OF NOTHINGNESS · ākiñcaññāyatanasappāyā paṭipadā
1ST WAY
dwell thus often
tabbahulavihārī
... and the perception of the imperturbable — all these are perceptions
kāmā, rūpā, yā ca āneñjasaññā — sabbā saññā
Where they cease without remainder, that is peaceful, that is sublime
aparisesā nirujjhanti etaṁ santaṁ etaṁ paṇītaṁ
When the mind acquires confidence · he attains to the base of nothingness
āyatane cittaṁ pasīdati · ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samāpajjati
2ND WAY
dwell thus often
tabbahulavihārī
When gone to the forest, root of a tree, or an empty hut — one reflects:
"This is empty of a self or of what belongs to a self"
suññamidaṁ attena vā attaniyena vā
When the mind acquires confidence · he attains to the base of nothingness
āyatane cittaṁ pasīdati · ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samāpajjati
3RD WAY
dwell thus often
tabbahulavihārī
"I do not belong to anyone anywhere"
nāhaṁ kvacani kassaci kiñcanatasmiṁ
"Nor does anything anywhere belong to me"
na ca mama kvacani kismiñci kiñcanaṁ natthī
When the mind acquires confidence · he attains to the base of nothingness
āyatane cittaṁ pasīdati · ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samāpajjati
CONDUCIVE TO NEITHER PERCEPTION NOR NON-PERCEPTION · nevasaññānāsaññāyatana
dwell thus often
tabbahulavihārī
... and the perception of the base of nothingness — all these are perceptions
kāmā, rūpā, yā ca āneñjasaññā, yā ca ākiñcaññāyatanasaññā — sabbā saññā
Where they cease without remainder, that is peaceful, that is sublime
aparisesā nirujjhanti etaṁ santaṁ etaṁ paṇītaṁ
When the mind acquires confidence · he attains to the base of neither perception nor non-perception
āyatane cittaṁ pasīdati · nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samāpajjati
NIBBĀNA
dwell thus often
tabbahulavihārī
Would such a bhikkhu attain Nibbāna or not?
"It might not be, and it might not be mine. It will not be, and it will not be mine
What there is, what has come to be—that I abandon"
no cassa, no ca me siyā; na bhavissati, na me bhavissati
yadatthi yaṁ bhūtaṁ—taṁ pajahāmī
Thus he acquires equanimity · so upekkhaṁ paṭilabhati
CLINGING
saupādāna
He delights in that equanimity, welcomes it, and holds tightly to it
So taṁ upekkhaṁ abhinandati, abhivadati, ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati
His consciousness becomes dependent on it and clings to it
tannissitaṁ hoti viññāṇaṁ tadupādānaṁ
A bhikkhu with clinging does not attain Nibbāna
Saupādāno bhikkhu na parinibbāyati
NON-CLINGING
anupādāna
He does not delight in that, does not welcome it, does not hold tightly to it
So taṁ upekkhaṁ nābhinandati, nābhivadati, na ajjhosāya tiṭṭhati
His consciousness does not become dependent on it and does not cling to it
na tannissitaṁ hoti viññāṇaṁ na tadupādānaṁ
A bhikkhu without clinging attains Nibbāna
anupādāno bhikkhu parinibbāyati
"The Blessed One has declared the crossing of the flood
by relying on one support after another"
nissāya nissāya kira bhagavatā oghassa nittharaṇā akkhātā
IDENTITY AS FAR AS IDENTITY EXTENDS
sakkāyapariyāpannaṁ
Perceptions of
Sensual pleasures · form · the imperturbable
the base of nothingness · neither perception nor non-perception
kāmā · rūpā · āneñjaṁ · ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ · nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ
DEATHLESS · amata
anupādā cittassa vimokkho
Deliverance of the mind through not clinging
The ending of craving · the fading away · cessation
taṇhākkhayo · virāgo · nirodho
"Meditate, Ānanda, do not be negligent; lest you later regret it."
jhāyathānanda, mā pamādattha, mā pacchā vippaṭisārino ahuvattha
